examples.title: Algorithm: Thread Creation with Mutex Sync
examples.description: Demonstrates basic thread creation and mutex synchronization to protect shared resources
examples.code:
ALGORITHM ThreadSafeCounter
BEGIN
 CREATE mutex
 INIT counter = 0

 FUNCTION worker_thread()
 BEGIN
 LOCK(mutex)
 counter += 1
 UNLOCK(mutex)
 END

 CREATE threads[2]
 FOR i = 0 TO 1
 threads[i] = CREATE_THREAD(worker_thread)

 FOR ALL threads
 JOIN_THREAD(thread)

 PRINT counter
END
examples.explanation:
1. CREATE mutex initializes synchronization primitive
2. LOCK/UNLOCK ensures atomic counter update
3. CREATE_THREAD spawns parallel execution
4. JOIN_THREAD ensures thread completion

examples.title: Pthreads Shared Counter
examples.description: C implementation using pthreads with mutex to prevent race conditions
examples.explanation:
1. pthread_mutex_init initializes mutex
2. pthread_create spawns threads
3. Mutex protects counter during increments
4. pthread_join waits for thread completion
5. Always destroy mutex after use

examples.title: Java Synchronized Threads
examples.description: Thread synchronization using Java's synchronized keyword
examples.explanation:
1. synchronized method provides intrinsic lock
2. Runnable interface enables thread creation
3. join() ensures main thread waits
4. Shared Counter instance enables thread communication

examples.title: Python Threading with Semaphore
examples.description: Resource access control using semaphores in Python
examples.code:
import threading

sem = threading.Semaphore(3)

def access_resource(thread_id):
 sem.acquire()
 print(f"Thread {thread_id} accessing resource")
 # Simulate work
 threading.Event().wait(0.5)
 print(f"Thread {thread_id} releasing resource")
 sem.release()

threads = []
for i in range(5):
 t = threading.Thread(target=access_resource, args=(i,))
 t.start()
 threads.append(t)

for t in threads:
 t.join()
examples.explanation:
1. Semaphore(3) allows 3 concurrent accesses
2. acquire()/release() manage permits
3. Event().wait() simulates processing time
4. Multiple threads compete for limited resources
